label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why smog has become a common concern. The options are A through D. First, I'll need to go back to the passage and find the relevant part. The passage mentions that smog is a common concern and gives a reason. Let me read through it again. The passage says: "Smog is especially a common concern. As a popular online post said, air may be the only thing that is equal for everyone, despite your income or profession. People with higher incomes are able to drink only bottled spring water and eat only organic food by paying higher prices, but they breathe the same air as everyone else." So the key point here is that no matter your income or profession, everyone breathes the same air. So even if you can pay more for clean water and food, you can't escape the air quality. That makes smog a universal issue, which is why it's a common concern. Looking at the options, option D says "Because nobody can avoid it." That aligns with the idea that everyone is equally affected by air quality, regardless of their status. Option B mentions a popular online post discussed it. While the passage does cite an online post, the reason given in the post is that air is equal for everyone, not just that it was discussed. The discussion is part of why it's a concern, but the actual reason is the inescapability. Option A talks about paying higher prices. The passage mentions that higher income people can pay for clean water and food, but air is the same for all. So the fact that people pay more for other things isn't why smog is a concern—it's the opposite, because you can't pay to avoid smog. Option C is about developing industry. The passage does mention economic development and the need for environmental protection, but the direct reason smog is a common concern isn't about
